article.title: Tracheobronchial transplantation with a stem-cell-seeded bioartificial nanocomposite: a proof-of-concept study
article.authors: Jungebluth P, Alici E, Baiguera S, Blomberg P, et al. (Macchiarini P)

study_type.notes: Case report of single patient (36-year-old male) with recurrent primary tracheal cancer treated with bioartificial tracheal transplant. n=1. RETRACTED - published Lancet 2011, retracted 2016 due to scientific misconduct and patient harm.

1.notes: Case selection criteria: 36-year-old male; recurrent primary tracheal cancer after previous debulking and radiation therapy; candidate for experimental bioartificial transplant

2.notes: Condition measurement: tracheal cancer confirmed by pathology; recurrence verified by imaging and endoscopy; bioartificial construct assessed via scanning electron microscopy and confocal microscopy

3.notes: Valid diagnostic methods: pathological confirmation of tracheal cancer; radiological imaging for staging/recurrence; endoscopic examination; laboratory methods (flow cytometry, gene expression analysis)

4.notes: Single case only; consecutive inclusion not applicable with n=1

5.notes: Complete inclusion: single identified patient meeting criteria; patient included in all reported analyses; no exclusions from analysis

6.notes: Demographics: 36-year-old male explicitly stated; gender and age documented; prior medical history provided

7.notes: Clinical information: history of tracheal cancer; prior debulking surgery and radiation therapy; recurrent disease; presenting complaint and clinical indication for transplant described

8.notes: Outcomes reported: bioartificial construct characteristics (extracellular matrix-like coating, cell proliferation); gene expression patterns; pathological findings from graft examination

9.notes: Site demographics: Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm; Advanced Center for Translational Regenerative Medicine; tertiary academic center; European Airway Institute setting

10.notes: Analysis appropriate: descriptive analysis of single case; laboratory findings presented (flow cytometry, microscopy, gene expression); qualitative assessment of graft characteristics
